title: astragalus michaelis
intro: Astragalus michaelis, also known as Milk Vetch, is a perennial plant species native to China that belongs to the legume family. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries, prized for its adaptogenic and immune-boosting properties, which enable the body to resist stress and promote overall health. The medicinal properties of Astragalus michaelis are attributed to its rich content of isoflavones, saponins, and polysaccharides, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial activities. Astragalus michaelis is often prepared as a decoction, tincture, or capsule, and is commonly used to support immune function, reduce fatigue, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, making it a valuable herbal remedy for maintaining well-being.
